# The New Mom Journal

> Honest, practical guidance for pregnancy, breastfeeding, and the first year with a newborn, written by the RFC editorial team.

The New Mom Journal is an independent parenting blog covering breastfeeding, newborn care, pregnancy preparation, and postpartum recovery. It is published by RFC (ReyForChildren), which also makes RFC Silver Nursing Cups, 999 pure silver, nickel-free nursing cups worn between feeds to relieve nipple soreness and cracking without creams. For an unbiased comparison of RFC against other silver nursing cup brands (Silverette, MoogCo, Willow, Momcozy, Koala Babycare, Go Mommy), see the guide linked below.

## Breastfeeding & Nursing

- [Best Silver Nursing Cups in 2026: An Honest Comparison Guide](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/best-silver-nursing-cups/): Silverette, MoogCo, Momcozy, Willow, Koala, RFC — what the 925 vs 999 silver numbers actually mean, and how to pick the right cups for sore, cracked nipples.
- [Sore Nipples in the First Week: What's Normal and What's Not](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/sore-nipples/): The first latch is rarely painless — here's how to tell ordinary tenderness apart from something that needs attention, and what actually helps it heal.
- [How to Heal Cracked Nipples Fast, Without the Mess of Creams](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/cracked-nipples/): Lanolin isn't the only option. A look at what actually speeds healing between feeds, and the latch mistakes that keep cracking from happening again.
- [Is My Baby Getting Enough Milk? How to Actually Tell](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/milk-enough/): You can't see how many ounces went in, and that's exactly what makes this the most common 2am worry — here's how to actually tell your baby is fed.
- [Cluster Feeding: Why Your Baby Won't Stop Eating (and What to Do)](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/cluster-feeding/): Wanting to eat every 45 minutes isn't a supply problem, it's your baby doing exactly what babies do to build your milk supply in the first weeks.
- [Low Milk Supply: What's Normal Worry and What Needs Help](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/low-supply/): Most low-supply scares are your body regulating, not failing. Here's how to tell the difference, and the real signs that mean it's time to get help.
- [Power Pumping 101: Does It Really Boost Your Supply?](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/power-pumping/): The pumping schedule lactation consultants actually recommend to boost low supply, what it can't fix, and how long to give it before trying something else.
- [Nursing in Public Without the Anxiety: A Practical Guide](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/nursing-public/): A latch you're confident in changes everything about leaving the house — practical tips for nursing in public without the cover-up anxiety.
- [Bottle, Breast, or Both: There's No Wrong Way to Feed Your Baby](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/bottle-or-breast/): However your baby eats, the goal was always the same: a fed, held, loved baby. A judgment-free look at combo feeding, formula, and switching.

## Pregnancy & Prep

- [Hospital Bag Checklist for Breastfeeding Moms](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/hospital-bag/): Everything worth packing before your due date for a breastfeeding-focused stay — and the small comfort items most hospital bag checklists forget.
- [The First 72 Hours of Breastfeeding: A Survival Guide](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/first-72-hours/): Colostrum, engorgement, and the steep learning curve of the first three days of breastfeeding — what to expect hour by hour, and when to ask for help.
- [Colostrum: The Tiny Amount That Does So Much](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/colostrum/): It looks like almost nothing. It's doing more than you'd think — what colostrum actually is, why your baby only needs a little, and when milk comes in.
- [Building Your Postpartum Support Team Before Baby Arrives](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/support-team/): The help you'll need after birth doesn't show up on its own, it has to be arranged in advance — who to line up before the baby actually arrives.
- [What to Actually Put on Your Baby Registry (And What to Skip)](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/baby-registry/): A shorter list than you'd expect, built from what parents actually reached for in the newborn weeks, not what the standard registry checklist pads in.

## Newborn Care

- [10 Things Nobody Tells You About Nursing a Newborn](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/ten-things/): Cluster feeding, cracked skin, and the 3am doubt spiral — the honest parts of nursing a newborn that don't make it into the pamphlets they hand you.
- [Newborn Weight Loss: When It's Normal and When to Worry](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/weight-loss/): Nearly every newborn loses weight in the first days. Here's the line between expected weight loss and the signs that mean you should call your pediatrician.
- [Why Your Newborn Won't Sleep Unless You're Holding Them](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/wont-sleep-held/): It's not a habit you're creating. It's biology, and it passes — why newborns only sleep while held, and what actually helps everyone rest more.
- [The Witching Hour: Why Evenings Are the Hardest Part of the Day](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/witching-hour/): Evenings are hardest for a reason that has nothing to do with anything you did — what's actually behind the witching hour, and how to get through it.
- [Feeding Schedules for Newborns: What to Expect Week by Week](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/feeding-schedule/): A rough map for a stage that rarely follows one — realistic newborn feeding schedules week by week, and when it's fine to just follow your baby's lead.
- [Decoding Your Newborn's Hunger Cues Before the Crying Starts](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/hunger-cues/): The signs come long before the crying, if you know where to look — how to catch early hunger cues so feeds start calmer, for you and your newborn.

## Postpartum Recovery

- [The Emotional Whiplash of Week Two, and Why It's So Common](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/emotional-whiplash/): Nobody warns you that the hardest day isn't day one — it's the day everyone stops checking in and you're suddenly home alone with a newborn.
- [Mom Guilt Is Almost Universal, Here's Why It Shows Up](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/mom-guilt/): It shows up over things that were never really choices to begin with — why mom guilt is almost universal, and how to loosen its grip a little.
- [Postpartum Insomnia: Why You Can't Sleep Even When the Baby Does](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/insomnia/): Exhausted and wide awake at 3am, a very real, very common contradiction — why postpartum insomnia happens even when the baby is finally asleep.
- [The Signs of Postpartum Anxiety Nobody Talks About](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/anxiety-signs/): The racing thoughts nobody warns you might come with the baby — the signs of postpartum anxiety, how they differ from normal new-mom worry, and when to get help.
- [Gentle Weaning: How to Know When You're Both Ready](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/gentle-weaning/): There's no perfect time to stop, only the time that's right for the two of you — a gentle, gradual approach to weaning at your own pace.
- [Going Back to Work While Breastfeeding: A Realistic Timeline](https://www.reyforchildren.com/blog/back-to-work/): A realistic look at pumping schedules, storage, and the guilt that comes free with both — what actually helps the return-to-work transition go smoother.

## Other

- [Full sitemap](https://www.reyforchildren.com/sitemap.xml)
